components: Chart: Total Workforce Growth (2006-2030) with confidence intervals
Chart: Multi-line workforce trends by profession
Chart: CAGR by profession (horizontal bar chart)

tabs_implemented.description: Forecasted workforce supply with confidence intervals
components: Chart: Forecast lines with 80% confidence intervals
Chart: Stacked area chart of public/private composition over time
Chart: Grouped bar chart of public vs private CAGR divergence

features_implemented: Interactive filtering: Profession selection across all tabs
Hover tooltips: Exact values displayed on all charts
Confidence intervals: 80% CI shaded areas on forecast charts
Data loading: Polars for efficient data processing
Logging: Loguru structured logging to dashboard.log
Error handling: Graceful degradation with error logging
Stacked area charts: Public/private composition visualization
Grouped bar charts: Sector comparison visualization

instructions_to_run.step_1: Ensure virtual environment is activated: source .venv/bin/activate
instructions_to_run.step_2: Install required packages: uv pip install plotly dash dash-bootstrap-components polars loguru
instructions_to_run.step_3: Navigate to problem statement directory: cd problem-statement/ps-001-healthcare-workforce-sustainability
instructions_to_run.step_4: Run the dashboard script: python src/analysis/dashboard_workforce.py
instructions_to_run.step_5: Open browser and navigate to: http://localhost:8050
instructions_to_run.alternative: Or run from workspace root: python problem-statement/ps-001-healthcare-workforce-sustainability/src/analysis/dashboard_workforce.py

notes_for_reviewer: All data paths use absolute paths from workspace root for reliability
Dashboard handles missing data gracefully with try-except blocks
Forecast confidence intervals (80% CI) calculated from model outputs
Public vs private analysis only available for professions with sector breakdown in historical data
All charts include hover tooltips with exact values
Dashboard logs startup information and errors to logs/dashboard.log
Profession names are formatted for display (e.g., 'advanced_practice_nurse' → 'Advanced Practice Nurse')
The dashboard auto-calculates KPIs from the data on each load
known_limitations: Export button functionality not implemented (requires additional callbacks)
Sector filter (public/private) in Tab 3 is UI-only, logic not fully implemented
No authentication/authorization (assumes internal MOH network deployment)
Data refresh requires dashboard restart (no auto-refresh mechanism)
Mobile responsiveness limited to tablet sizes and above
next_steps_for_deployment: Add help tooltips explaining metrics (CAGR, YoY, CI)
Integrate MOH SSO authentication if deploying to production
Configure Databricks Dashboard hosting if platform chosen
Add data refresh mechanism or schedule
